Strong Fit

Software Engineer

Bright Purple Resourcing
South East London (SE1), SE1
£40,000 - £60,000
Software Developer - London, Hybrid - A growing professional services business in London is looking for a talented Software Developer to join their Technology and Data team. This is a hands-on, end-to-end role for someone who takes genuine satisfaction in building software that people actually use, production systems that make a real difference to how a business operates, not prototypes that never see the light of day. * At least one to two years of commercial software development experience - In many organisations, a developer at this stage of their career ends up siloed, one layer of the stack, one corner of the product, limited visibility of the bigger picture.

Corriculo Ltd
Camden, North West London (NW1), N1C

If you are an experienced AI Software developer, keen to progress into a CTO role and would like to learn more, please apply below and I will be in touch with you shortly to discuss the role further - An exceptional opportunity for an AI Software Engineer to join an early stage start-up, building a workflow and intelligence platform powered by agentic AI, multimodal analysis and domain-specific insight. Building on Firebase, using Claude API and leading LLMs, the AI Software Engineer will build the end-to-end architecture, security, performance and integration. Working closely with the Head of Product, the AI Software Engineer will take well-defined specs and prototypes (Replit) and turn them into production-quality code. As the company grows, the AI Software Engineer will start building the engineering function - hiring, structuring, mentoring.

  1. Jobs
  2. London
  3. Software Developer

What is the average salary for Software Developer in London?

Average salary per year
£62,499
The average salary for a Software Developer in London is £62,499. Software Developer in London salaries range from £52,499 to £77,499.

Find out more

How many Software Developer jobs are there in London?
There are 900 Software Developer jobs within 10 miles of London available on Totaljobs right now.
Where in London are these jobs?
You can find jobs in City of London, South East London, South West London.
What other similar jobs are there to Software Developer jobs in London?
As well as Software Developer jobs in London, you can find Software Engineer, Software Solutions Developer, Coder, amongst many others.
Which industry do Software Developer jobs in London belong to?
Software Developer jobs in London are part of the IT industry.
How many temporary and part-time Software Developer jobs are available in London?
There are 6 temporary Software Developer jobs and 69 part-time Software Developer jobs available in London at the moment.
Essential skills for a software developer career
Aspiring software developers should focus on mastering programming languages like C#, JavaScript, and Python. Proficiency in .NET, ReactJS, and SQL, alongside strong problem-solving abilities and familiarity with Agile methods, are crucial for success in this field.
A typical day in the life of a software developer professional
Software engineers typically start their day reviewing code and attending team meetings to discuss project progress. They spend most of their time writing and testing code, collaborating with colleagues, and documenting their work for future reference.
How to start a career in software development?
Begin by earning a degree in computer science or a related field, or completing a coding bootcamp. Gain practical experience through internships or personal projects, and build a portfolio showcasing your skills in web development and software engineering.
What are the different roles in the software development industry?
The industry offers diverse roles, from junior software developer to senior software developer positions. Specialisations include front-end development, database management, and software architecture, with opportunities to progress to roles like software programmer or graduate software engineer.

The Stepstone Group UK Ltd © 2026

We use cookies

We use cookies to operate our website, measure how people use our services to help us improve them, enrich usage profiles and personalise the content displayed to you. We also share information about your use of our site with our social media, advertising, and analytics partners, who may combine it with other information, to show you relevant ads on other websites, and to measure the performance of our advertising.

Some cookies are essential for the operation of our website. If you would prefer us not to use the non-essential cookies, click “Just necessary”, but please be aware that this may impact your experience of our site and the services we are able to offer. Click “Accept” if you’re happy for us to place these cookies. You can change your mind at any time.

Cookies, device identifiers, or other information can be stored or accessed on your device for the purposes presented to you.
Ads and content can be personalised based on a profile. More data can be added to better personalise ads and content. The performance of ads and content can be measured. Insights about audiences who have viewed the ads and content can be extracted. Data can be used to build or improve user experience, systems and software.
Content from social networks and embedded content from third-party providers can be displayed.
If an identifier is created based on authentication data, such as contact details associated with online accounts you have set up on websites or apps (e.g. email address, phone number) or customer identifiers (e.g. an identifier provided by your telecommunications provider), then this identifier can be used to recognize you across websites, apps and devices if you are logged in with the same contact details.
You can also exercise your right to object to data processing based on legitimate interests under “Privacy Policy”.
Privacy PolicyCookie policy
